summary: Officially documented TDR timeout threshold reset. This app write restores TdrDelay to Microsoft's documented default of 2 seconds, but Microsoft says TDR keys are for targeted driver testing and debugging rather than normal end-user tuning.

setting.name: Graphics TDR Delay
setting.casual_explanation: Windows uses TDR to detect when the GPU takes too long to respond. This setting controls the main timeout threshold before recovery starts.
setting.why_people_change_it: People usually touch it only when undoing manual GPU timeout experiments or driver debugging changes.
setting.tradeoffs_overview: Restoring the documented default is useful when undoing manual graphics-driver tweaks, but Microsoft does not recommend normal users manipulate TDR keys as a routine optimization.
setting.professional_notes: Microsoft documents TdrDelay under HKLM\System\CurrentControlSet\Control\GraphicsDrivers and states that the default value is 2 seconds. The same Microsoft page warns that these registry keys are for testing or debugging during driver development and should not be manipulated by end users as general tuning controls.

allowed_values.label: Microsoft default timeout
allowed_values.meaning: The GPU timeout threshold is set to Microsoft's documented default of 2 seconds.

app_current_implementation.notes: The current app writes TdrDelay = 2 on the Microsoft-documented GraphicsDrivers path.

windows_defaults.label: Microsoft documented default
windows_defaults.applies_to: Systems restoring the documented TDR delay baseline

states.rationale: Microsoft documents 2 seconds as the default TdrDelay value.

windows_defaults.notes: This record is for restoring the documented baseline rather than recommending routine end-user tuning.

recommended_profiles.label: Reset to Microsoft default
intended_for: Systems returning to the documented baseline

states.rationale: Restore the documented default timeout threshold.

recommended_profiles.good_when: You are undoing manual TDR edits and want the documented default back.
recommended_profiles.bad_when: You are treating TDR keys as a casual performance tweak.
tradeoffs: The documented Microsoft default is restored.
TDR keys remain an advanced debugging area, not a general-user tuning surface.

evidence.title: Microsoft Learn: Testing and debugging TDR during driver development
evidence.summary: Microsoft documents TdrDelay under HKLM\System\CurrentControlSet\Control\GraphicsDrivers and states that the default value is 2 seconds, while also warning that TDR keys are for targeted driver testing and debugging only.

evidence.title: Current app implementation
evidence.summary: The provider writes TdrDelay = 2 for the current tweak.

decision.why: Microsoft documents the exact path and default value, and the app writes that documented default as a reset-to-baseline action with a clean rollback story.

validation_proof.exact_quote_or_path: KeyPath : HKEY_LOCAL_MACHINE\System\CurrentControlSet\Control\GraphicsDrivers; KeyValue : TdrDelay; ValueType : REG_DWORD; ValueData : Number of seconds to delay. The default value is 2 seconds.
validation_proof.notes: Microsoft's TDR registry keys page documents the exact GraphicsDrivers runtime path, the TdrDelay value name, and the documented default of 2 seconds.
